errors: The ELM implementation has an age threshold mismatch. The CPG specifies an age range of 30-85 years, but the ELM implementation uses an age range of 30-80 years. The LDL Cholesterol threshold of 190 mg/dL matches the CPG requirement. However, the age threshold discrepancy needs to be corrected to exactly match the CPG requirements.

The HbA1c Laboratory Test value in the ELM implementation is specified as >= 9 g%, whereas the CPG requirements specify it as >= 6.5%. This is a mismatch in the numeric value. Additionally, the ELM implementation mentions a "9 g%" which seems to be an incorrect unit for HbA1c, as it is typically measured as a percentage (%). The CPG requirements correctly specify the unit as %.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG specifies an age range of 65 to 75 years, but the ELM implementation checks for an age range of 55 to 75 years. The logical structure and value set references appear to be correct, but this numeric value mismatch needs to be corrected to exactly match the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation of the MeetsInclusionCriteria logic is incorrect. The CPG requires that all of the following must be true: Screening Age (age >= 40 years), Gender (female), and Mammogram (no procedure in past 1 year). However, the ELM implementation uses an OR operator instead of an AND operator. The correct implementation should be: [MeetsInclusionCriteria] = [Screening Age] AND [Gender] AND [Mammogram]. Additionally, the Mammogram logic in ELM is IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...)), which checks if there is no mammogram procedure in the lookback period, but the CPG requires that there should be no mammogram procedure in the past 1 year, which can be correctly implemented using the IsNull function as written, but the description of the logic should be carefully reviewed to ensure it aligns with the CPG's intent. The ELM implementation description should be carefully compared to the CPG to ensure all aspects are correctly implemented.

The ELM implementation contains errors in the logical structure. Specifically, the "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" definition is incorrect. It should be "[In Demographic] AND NOT [Has Recent Falls Assessment]" instead of "[In Demographic] AND [Has Recent Falls Assessment]". This is because the CPG requires that no falls risk assessment has been performed in the past 1 year for screening to be needed. Additionally, the "InPopulation" definition should be "[In Demographic] AND NOT [Has Recent Falls Assessment]" to correctly implement the CPG decision logic. The "Recommendation", "Rationale", and "Indicator" definitions also need to be updated to reflect the correct logical structure.

The ELM implementation references a different ValueSet for "Fall Risk Assessment" (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564) than the one specified in the CPG (2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028). This discrepancy may affect the accuracy of the falls prevention screening decision logic.
The ELM implementation references a different value set for "Alcohol Use Screening" (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278) than the one specified in the CPG (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542).
